name: Panoramic route of Turó l'Home – circular from Sant Celoni through the Montseny Natural Park
name_original: Ruta panorámica de Turó l'Home – circular desde Sant Celoni por el Parque Natural de Montseny
region_intro: Road cycling around Sant Celoni benefits from its location at the foothills of Montseny Natural Park and Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park. The terrain features significant elevation changes, with routes winding through lush forests and offering panoramic views. Cyclists can expect a mix of challenging ascents and quiet roads, providing diverse options for various skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Sant Celoni The most popular road cycling route is Panoramic route of Turó l'Home – circular from Sant Celoni through the Montseny Natural Park, a 17.1 miles (27.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views within the Montseny Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Roca Road – Pedret Bridge loop from Estació de Gualba, a difficult 112.1 miles (180.4 km) path. This long-distance route features substantial elevation gain and varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Santa Fe Reservoir – Mirador de Les Goitadores loop from Sant Celoni, a 32.1 miles (51.7 km) trail leading through the Montseny area, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Celoni is defined by challenging ascents, quiet forest roads, and views of the Montseny and Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Sant Celoni are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Celoni's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Celoni?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Sant Celoni, offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate loops to challenging long-distance rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Sant Celoni?
A: The terrain around Sant Celoni features significant elevation changes, as it's situated at the foothills of Montseny Natural Park and Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park. Expect winding roads through lush forests, challenging ascents, and panoramic views. Roads are generally well-maintained, especially during weekdays.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the Sant Celoni area?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging ascents. Notable climbs include the demanding ascent to &lt;strong&gt;Turó de l'Home&lt;/strong&gt;, one of Catalonia's steepest roads, and &lt;strong&gt;Coll Formic&lt;/strong&gt;. The climb to &lt;strong&gt;Santa Fe del Montseny&lt;/strong&gt; is also a popular and scenic option. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16052790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m) – Montseny Forest Road loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gain.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes around Sant Celoni?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are options suitable for beginners. You can find easier sections within the natural parks, particularly on less steep roads. Look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances to start.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes from Sant Celoni?
A: Absolutely. Sant Celoni serves as an excellent base for longer rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Roca Road – Pedret Bridge loop from Estació de Gualba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 112.1-mile (180.4 km) path featuring substantial elevation gain and varied landscapes, perfect for experienced long-distance cyclists.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Sant Celoni?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995566070/panoramic-route-of-turo-l-home-circular-from-sant-celoni-through-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic route of Turó l'Home – circular from Sant Celoni through the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.1-mile (27.6 km) trail.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through or near significant natural and historical sites. In Montseny Natural Park, you can encounter peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/988421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Agudes&lt;/a&gt;. Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park offers views of the Maresme coast. You might also spot historical elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1296433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Pedra Gentil&lt;/a&gt;, ancient megalithic tombs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Celoni?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with forests changing dramatically with the seasons. Summers can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling, particularly on sunny days.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Sant Celoni?
A: Sant Celoni itself offers various parking options, and many routes start directly from the town. For routes beginning deeper within the natural parks, designated parking areas are typically available near trailheads or popular starting points. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended parking locations.

Q: Is public transport accessible for reaching road cycling routes in Sant Celoni?
A: Sant Celoni has a train station, making it accessible by public transport. From there, you can easily start many road cycling routes directly or connect to nearby areas. However, always check local public transport regulations regarding bicycles, especially during peak hours.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Sant Celoni?
A: The road cycling routes around Sant Celoni are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the quiet roads, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the stunning views within the Montseny and Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Parks.

Q: Are there less crowded road cycling options around Sant Celoni?
A: While popular, the extensive network of roads, especially within the natural parks, often allows for less crowded cycling, particularly during weekdays or outside of peak tourist seasons. Exploring routes that venture deeper into Montseny or Montnegre i el Corredor can lead to quieter experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that include coffee stops or places to rest?
A: Many villages and towns along the routes, particularly within the natural parks, offer cafes, restaurants, and small shops where you can refuel and rest. Specific routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26915434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Reservoir – Mirador de Les Goitadores loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt; pass through areas where such stops can be found, allowing for a pleasant break during your ride.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring historical sites in the area?
A: Yes, the natural surroundings of Sant Celoni are rich in historical elements. You can cycle past Neolithic dolmens like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1296433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes&lt;/a&gt;, Iberian archaeological remains, and various medieval churches and hermitages, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.
tips:
Santa María de Palautordera is a municipality in Catalonia, Spain. It belongs to the province of Barcelona, specifically to the region of Vallés Oriental. It is part of the Bajo Montseny subregion.

   It is located in the Tordera river valley, at the foot of the Montseny mountain range. The predominant vegetation are pine and oak forests.
Road junction from Mosqueroles to Montseny with Costa del Montseny, where you can continue ascending towards Turó de l'home and Santa Fé, or towards Collformic and Seva.
Forest road with little traffic. If you continue you can reach the top of Turo de l'home.
Straight with the whole of Montseny where you can see El turó de l'home, Collformic, and all the mountains of the Natural Park in the background.

waypoints.name: Straight section with views of Montseny
